doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m glad to be here, Dr. Lee. So, I’ve been having this pain in my left elbow for about three months now. It started off kinda mild, but it’s been getting worse, especially in the last few weeks. It’s mostly achy, but sometimes it gets sharp, and it really hurts when I extend my elbow or do certain movements. I’m honestly a bit worried about it.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your elbow?
patient_turn: Uh, I first noticed it about three months ago. It was just a little discomfort at first, you know? But, um, over time it’s really intensified, especially in the last three weeks. It’s been pretty hard to deal with, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Can you point to exactly where the pain is located in your elbow?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, the pain is mostly on the medial side of my left elbow. It’s like, right around there, and it gets worse with certain movements, like when I extend my arm or, um, when I flex my wrist. It’s really frustrating.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like—sharp, dull, achy, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s mostly achy, but, um, sometimes it gets sharp, especially if I move my arm in a certain way. I’d say it’s like a 5 out of 10 in terms of severity. It’s just, you know, really annoying and makes it hard to do my job sometimes.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your elbow?
How has the pain changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Well, um, it started off pretty mild, but it’s definitely gotten worse over time. Like, in the last three weeks, it’s been really intense, and I feel it more after my shifts at work. I’m kinda worried that it’s not getting any better, you know?

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your elbow?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started maybe three months ago, but I’m not totally sure. I’ve been working a lot on the assembly line, and it gets worse after shifts, you know? Also, I’ve been trying to remember if I did anything different around that time, like, I bought a new set of tools, which could be part of it.

doctor_turn: Does the pain stay in your elbow, or does it spread to other areas?
patient_turn: Um, the pain pretty much stays in my elbow. It’s mostly localized on the medial side, and it doesn’t really spread anywhere else. But, you know, it gets worse with certain movements, which makes it really annoying.

doctor_turn: Have you ever injured your elbow in the past?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any injuries to my elbow before this. I mean, I’ve been pretty healthy overall, so this is kinda new for me. It’s just frustrating because I don’t really know what’s causing it.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any swelling or redness around your elbow?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I haven’t noticed any swelling or redness around my elbow. It’s just, um, painful and tender to touch, but there’s no visible swelling or anything like that. I’m kinda worried because it just doesn’t seem to be getting better.

doctor_turn: Have you felt any numbness or tingling in your arm or hand?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t felt any numbness or tingling in my arm or hand. It’s just the pain in my elbow that’s been bothering me. I’m honestly a bit worried since it’s been going on for a while now.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any fever, weight loss, or unusual fatigue lately?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t experienced any fever, weight loss, or unusual fatigue. I mean, I’ve been feeling pretty normal otherwise, except for this elbow pain. It’s just, um, really frustrating to deal with.

doctor_turn: What is your living situation like, and do you have any daily habits or routines?
patient_turn: I live with my partner, and, um, I work on an assembly line, which involves a lot of repetitive tasks. I don’t smoke, but I do have a glass of wine every other day and use cannabis weekly. My daily routine is pretty busy, but I try to manage everything, you know? It’s just been tough with this elbow pain lately.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of elbow or joint problems?
patient_turn: Um, no, there’s no family history of elbow or joint problems that I know of. My family hasn’t had any issues like that, so it’s kinda surprising that I’m dealing with this now. I’m just trying to figure out what’s going on.

doctor_turn: Does taking any pain relievers help relieve your elbow pain?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, taking NSAIDs does help a bit. It temporarily relieves the pain, but it doesn’t last long, you know? I still feel it pretty strongly when I’m doing certain movements, so it’s not a complete solution.

doctor_turn: What did the doctor say they found during your physical examination?
patient_turn: Well, um, the doctor found tenderness over the medial elbow and olecranon. They noticed that I had pain during specific movements, like resisted wrist flexion and passive wrist extension. But there wasn’t any swelling or warmth in the joint, which was a bit of a relief, I guess.
